title: Inventory temperature reanalysis, temperature-mortality studies, housing-quality surveys, and heating-access data sources for LMIC cold-exposed regions
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates temperature reanalysis products (ERA5-Land), temperature-mortality studies, DHS housing-quality data, and heating-access indicators into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit LMIC and high-altitude coverage assessment.
inputs: ERA5-Land temperature reanalysis documentation and validation studies
Gasparrini et al. 2015 and subsequent cold-mortality studies
DHS housing module documentation for cold-relevant indicators
Published cold-mortality studies from LMIC high-altitude regions
Heating-access and energy-poverty data from national surveys
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic coverage, and documented limitations — particularly temperature-reanalysis validation in high-altitude LMIC regions and housing-proxy correlation with indoor temperature.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering resolution, LMIC coverage, high-altitude validation status, and proxy-limitation documentation.

title: Map cold-spell frequency and intensity at sub-national scale for at least two LMIC high-altitude or cold-exposed regions
outcome: A sub-national cold-spell frequency and intensity analysis for at least two LMIC regions using ERA5-Land daily minimum temperature, documenting cold-spell definition methodology, altitude-related temperature patterns, and reanalysis-resolution limitations in mountainous terrain.
inputs: ERA5-Land daily minimum temperature data for target regions
Digital elevation model data for altitude context
Cold-spell definition methodology from published studies
Ground weather-station data for validation where available
expected_artifact: validation.md update with cold-spell methodology and regional results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms ERA5-Land data extraction, cold-spell definition methodology, and altitude-context documentation, with explicit validation against ground stations where available.
done_condition: Cold-spell analysis includes methodology, regional results, altitude-context documentation, ERA5-Land resolution limitations, and replication status for at least two regions.

title: Map heating-access vulnerability using DHS housing-quality indicators and energy-poverty data for target regions
outcome: A heating-access vulnerability analysis combining cold-spell frequency with DHS housing-quality indicators and energy-poverty data, with explicit documentation that housing-material proxies have not been validated against indoor temperature measurements.
inputs: DHS housing-quality data for target regions
Energy-poverty and heating-fuel data from national surveys
Published studies on housing materials and indoor temperature
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with vulnerability methodology and proxy-limitation documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms vulnerability-weighting methodology, housing-quality proxy integration, and proxy-limitation documentation for each target region.
done_condition: Vulnerability analysis includes methodology, regional results, housing-proxy limitations, energy-poverty context, and replication status.

title: Red-team cold-mortality extrapolation overstatement and relative-priority risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how cold-mortality risk estimates could overstate the burden by extrapolating from high-income country studies, how results could be misused to deprioritize heat-adaptation investments, and how housing-quality proxies could misclassify vulnerability.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every cold-mortality risk claim has extrapolation-assumption documentation, housing-proxy limitations, and altitude-context caveats. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every cold-mortality risk claim is either blocked, softened to proxy-limited language with explicit extrapolation caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match housing, energy, and health authority decision needs in cold-exposed LMIC regions
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the cold-mortality vulnerability analysis would change any real decision for housing ministries, energy-access programs, or health authorities in high-altitude LMIC regions without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for housing, energy, and health audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that cold-mortality estimates are used to deprioritize heat adaptation, or that housing-proxy vulnerability maps misclassify actual indoor-temperature exposure. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Nepal Ministry of Health cold-weather preparedness officer), named decision (e.g., high-altitude district health resource allocation), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for deprioritizing heat adaptation, not for heating-fuel subsidy justification without affordability analysis), and replicated field-reality review.
